BTS · 쩔어 · Choreography Breakdown

DOPE
쩔어

Jin Part (7) + Jungkook Part
SongDOPE (쩔어)
AlbumHYYH Pt. 1 · 2015
Parts#7 Jin + JK
Tempo~138 BPM
📱 How to use Tap any count card to expand for body position details + a highlighted frame from your instructor's video. Desktop is best for detail — but mobile works! Notes field at the bottom is editable.

Jin Part · 소리쳐봐 all right

📍 Timestamp ~2:01 → 2:14 Confirm with instructor's counted video
한국어
소리쳐봐 all right
몸이 타버리도록 all night
(all night)
Phonetic
So-ri-chyeo-bwa all right
Mo-mi ta-beo-ri-do-rok all night
(all night)
English
Make some noise, all right
Till your body burns up, all night
(echo)
Reference · Jin Part counts 1–8 (tap to enlarge)
Reference · Jin Part contd counts 1–8 (tap to enlarge)

Count-by-Count · Jin (8-count phrase 1)

1
소- · So- Side-on stance, R hand raised overhead
▼
COUNT 1
👟 Feet

Standing side-on, feet roughly hip-width. Weight on left foot, right slightly forward. Facing R side of room.

🙌 Body / Arms

Torso angled to the right. Right arm raised up — elbow bent, hand near face/head. Left arm lower, relaxed curve.

✋ Hands

Right hand relaxed, fingers loose near cheek/ear height. Active not stiff — this is the "call out" position.

Korean
소리쳐봐
Phonetic
So-ri-chyeo-bwa
English
Make some noise
2
리 · -ri- R arm starts to lower, body stays side-on
▼
COUNT 2
👟 Feet

Still side-on, similar stance. Weight begins shifting — slight lean into the phrase.

🙌 Body / Arms

R arm lowers slightly from count 1. Torso still angled right. Energy flowing through — no dead arms.

✋ Hands

R hand coming down, fingers still active. Keep awareness in the hands between counts.

Korean
소리쳐봐
Phonetic
so-RI-chyeo-bwa
English
make some noise
3
쳐 · -chyeo- · ACCENT Sharp turn, both arms engage
▼
COUNT 3
👟 Feet

Beginning to turn/shift toward front. This is the accent syllable — foot placement sharpens here.

🙌 Body / Arms

Both arms now engaged. Sharp energy — "쳐" means to strike. Let the body reflect that pop. Chest opens slightly.

✋ Hands

Sharpness here — this is the hit count. Hands reflect the accent. Clean and decisive.

Korean
쳐봐
Phonetic
CHYEO-bwa
English
noise → all right
4
봐 all right · resolve Facing more front, arms in wide low hold
▼
COUNT 4
👟 Feet

More front-facing. Stance opens wider — strong base for the "all right" resolve.

🙌 Body / Arms

Arms drop to a wide, low hold. Body settling into the phrase resolution. This is a swagger hold — don't rush through it.

✋ Hands

Relaxed but held. Extend the "all right" — this is a confidence pose. Own it.

Korean
봐 all right
Phonetic
bwa — all right
English
— all right
5
몸이 · Mo-mi · new phrase Step R, weight shifts, arm raises again
▼
COUNT 5
👟 Feet

Step right — new phrase opens with a weight shift. Energy resets with this step.

🙌 Body / Arms

R arm rises again, mirroring the phrase structure of count 1. "몸이" = "body" — the whole body speaks.

✋ Hands

Fresh energy up. R hand active near face/ear height again. Don't go passive at the start of phrase 2.

Korean
몸이 타버리도록
Phonetic
Mo-mi ta-beo-ri-do-rok
English
Till your body burns up
6
타 · ta- · intensity builds Side-on again, arm lowers with swagger
▼
COUNT 6
👟 Feet

Side-on again. Weight transfers building toward the "all night" peak. Dynamic foot energy here.

🙌 Body / Arms

"타버리도록" = "until it burns up." Arms building heat — more expansive. Don't flatten the energy mid-phrase.

✋ Hands

Active arms goal applies strongly here. Between "타" and "all night" is where arms tend to go dead — fight that.

Korean
타버리도록
Phonetic
ta-beo-ri-do-rok
English
burns up
7
all night · PEAK Step L, full side-on, arms in low hold
▼
COUNT 7
👟 Feet

Step left. Strong ground — "all night" is the peak of this phrase. Plant with intention.

🙌 Body / Arms

Full side-on, arms in a wide low hold. This is where confidence and swagger lives — feel it. Big held moment.

✋ Hands

Bold position — held, not passive. This is a held moment. Make it count on stage.

Korean
all night
Phonetic
all night
English
all night · peak 🔥
8
(all night) · echo → JK Step into transition, don't drop early
▼
COUNT 8
👟 Feet

Transition step — setting up for JK's "Cause we got fire." Don't drop energy early here.

🙌 Body / Arms

Echo energy — slightly softer than count 7 but still intentional. Land cleanly, don't collapse.

✋ Hands

Resolve the phrase. Clean finish = better handoff to JK's entrance.

Korean
(all night)
Phonetic
(all night) echo
English
echo → into JK

Count-by-Count · Jin (8-count phrase 2)

1
몸이 · facing front Feet together, arms low and crossed
▼
COUNT 1
👟 Feet

Facing front, feet roughly together or narrow stance. Strong upright posture.

🙌 Body / Arms

Arms low and close to body — almost crossed at the wrists in front. Loading position before the big sweep.

✋ Hands

Hands low, pulled in — think compressed spring. The arms are about to explode open.

Korean
몸이
Phonetic
mo-mi
English
body
2
타- · arms begin to sweep out Feet wider, arms opening outward from chest
▼
COUNT 2
👟 Feet

Feet beginning to widen into a strong stance. Weight distributes evenly as arms spread.

🙌 Body / Arms

Arms sweeping out and up from the chest-cross position. Big expanding energy — this is where the phrase opens up.

✋ Hands

Fingers extending outward. This sweep needs active hands — not floppy wrists.

Korean
타버리
Phonetic
ta-beo-ri
English
burns
3
도록 · arms wide open Wide stance, arms fully extended to sides
▼
COUNT 3
👟 Feet

Wide stance — grounded and powerful. Feet firmly planted to support the big arm spread.

🙌 Body / Arms

Arms fully extended wide — like wings spreading. This is a huge shape. Chest open, shoulders back. Don't shrink.

✋ Hands

Fingers extended out to the sides. Full reach — make yourself as wide as possible.

Korean
도록 all night
Phonetic
do-rok all night
English
up, all night
4
all night · jazz hands UP Both arms swing up high, jazz hands
▼
COUNT 4
👟 Feet

Still wide — strong base needed for the arms going overhead. Don't let your stance collapse as arms go up.

🙌 Body / Arms

BOTH arms swing up high above head. This is the biggest shape in the sequence. Full jazz energy — this is THE moment.

✋ Hands

JAZZ HANDS — fingers spread wide and active up high. This is your active arms goal in full expression.

Korean
all night ✨
Phonetic
all night
English
all night — peak arms!
5
all night · arms pull in Arms bend in toward chest, still jazz hands
▼
COUNT 5
👟 Feet

Wide stance continues. Solid base as arms pull in from the overhead position.

🙌 Body / Arms

Arms pull in and bend — elbows come toward the body, hands still spread. Still jazz energy but gathering in.

✋ Hands

Jazz hands still active — fingers spread. Don't drop the hand energy as arms come in.

Korean
(all night)
Phonetic
(all night)
English
echo / resolve
6
arms tuck low Arms pulling down, hands at chest/hip level
▼
COUNT 6
👟 Feet

Still wide, possibly with a slight knee bend — setting up for the final phrase. Stay grounded.

🙌 Body / Arms

Arms coming down from the jazz overhead, bending at elbows. Hands tracking down toward chest/hip height.

✋ Hands

Fingers still active — don't go limp as you descend. Keep the tension in the hands through the transition.

Korean
transition
Phonetic
—
English
bridge to finale
7
hands chest-level, elbows out Wide stance, arms bent out at sides
▼
COUNT 7
👟 Feet

Wide strong stance. Elbows out wide — take up space. This is a powerful held shape.

🙌 Body / Arms

Arms bent at elbow, hands at chest level, elbows flared out to the sides. Compact but powerful. V-shape energy.

✋ Hands

Fingers curved/active at chest height. This is a strong held position — not passive.

Korean
—
Phonetic
—
English
held position
8
hands flick out → JK enters Sharp outward flick, wide stance — handoff!
▼
COUNT 8
👟 Feet

Wide stance, strong landing. This is the final resolution before JK explodes in.

🙌 Body / Arms

Arms flick out sharply to the sides — a final punctuation. Then JK's "Cause we got fire" begins immediately.

✋ Hands

Sharp outward flick — this is the cleanest transition moment. Nail it and JK's entrance feels electric.

Korean
→ JK!
Phonetic
handoff
English
→ Cause we got fire!
JUNGKOOK ↓
Follows directly after Jin
📍 Timestamp ~2:14 → 2:26 Confirm with instructor's counted video

Cause we got fire
Higher
I gotta make it ×2
쩔어!
Phonetic
Cause we got fire
Higher
I gotta make it ×2
Jjeo-reo!
English
Cause we got fire (+ Jimin echo)
Higher ↑ (+ Jimin echo)
I gotta make it ×2
Dope! / It's sick!
Reference · Jungkook Part counts 1–8 (tap to enlarge)
Reference · Jungkook Part contd counts 1–8 (tap to enlarge)

Count-by-Count · Jungkook (8-count phrase 1)

1
Cause we— · wide open stance Wide legs, L arm out low, R arm sweeps back
▼
COUNT 1
👟 Feet

Wide open stance — feet well apart. Weight balanced, knees soft. Strong wide base from the start.

🙌 Body / Arms

L arm extended out low to the left. R arm sweeping back/behind. Body open — this entrance has immediate presence.

✋ Hands

Both hands active and extended. Open, reaching shapes — this is the swagger entrance. No passive arms from count 1.

Lyric
Cause we got
Phonetic
Cause we got
English
Cause we got—
2
fire! 🔥 · arms cross body Arms pull into cross-body, lean in
▼
COUNT 2
👟 Feet

Feet staying wide. Body leans slightly forward as arms cross — grounded base supports the lean.

🙌 Body / Arms

Both arms pull in and cross at the body — elbows in, arms overlapping in front. Sharp, compact energy. This is "fire" — hit it.

✋ Hands

Hands active in the cross position. Jimin echoes "fire!" — use that group energy to sharpen your hit.

Lyric
fire! 🔥
Phonetic
fire!
English
fire! (+ Jimin echo)
3
Higher ↑ · arms sweep wide open Arms explode open to sides, chest open
▼
COUNT 3
👟 Feet

Wide stance holds. Body opens up fully as arms explode. Chest forward and open.

🙌 Body / Arms

Arms explode wide open from the cross — maximum wingspan. This is "Higher" — the word and the shape match. Go BIG.

✋ Hands

Full extension outward. Fingers spread. Jimin echoes "higher!" — this is a huge group moment. Ride it.

Lyric
Higher ↑
Phonetic
Higher
English
Higher (+ Jimin echo)
4
I gotta— · R arm punches up high One leg lifts, R arm punches up overhead
▼
COUNT 4
👟 Feet

One leg lifts — either a kick or knee lift. Balance on the standing leg. This is a dynamic balance moment.

🙌 Body / Arms

R arm punches straight UP overhead. L arm counterbalances lower. Full vertical contrast — this is the highest point of the whole JK phrase.

✋ Hands

R hand punches up clean — fist or pointed. Make this the sharpest moment in the phrase.

Lyric
I gotta make it
Phonetic
I gotta make it
English
I gotta make it
5
make it— · L arm sweeps across Foot lands, L arm sweeps across body
▼
COUNT 5
👟 Feet

Foot lands from count 4 — re-establishing the wide base. Weight plants back down with control.

🙌 Body / Arms

L arm sweeps across the body in a wide arc. R arm comes down from the punch. Cross-body sweep adds momentum.

✋ Hands

L hand active through the sweep — not floppy. This bridge move feeds directly into the second "I gotta make it."

Lyric
I gotta make it ×2
Phonetic
I gotta make it
English
I gotta make it ×2
6
make it— · both arms rise together Wide stance, both arms rise up together
▼
COUNT 6
👟 Feet

Wide stance firmly planted. Both arms going up needs a strong grounded base.

🙌 Body / Arms

Both arms lift and open upward — building toward the 쩔어 finale. This is the second wave of energy rising.

✋ Hands

Arms spread as they rise — fingers reaching outward and up. Keep building — don't plateau before 쩔어.

Lyric
I gotta make it
Phonetic
I gotta make it
English
building to 쩔어
7
jjeoreo— · arms fully raised Both arms at peak height, wide stance
▼
COUNT 7
👟 Feet

Wide strong stance. Both arms need grounding beneath them — don't let feet drift inward.

🙌 Body / Arms

Both arms raised high and wide — maximum spread. Runway into 쩔어. Hold the shape and let it land.

✋ Hands

Full reach upward and out. This is the runway — 쩔어 is one count away. COMMIT to this shape.

Lyric
I gotta make it—
Phonetic
I gotta make it—
English
→ 쩔어 in 1!
8
— · landing prep Body loading down for the final hit
▼
COUNT 8
👟 Feet

Loading down — slight knee bend or weight shift as body prepares for 쩔어's landing. Spring loading.

🙌 Body / Arms

Body compresses slightly — coiling energy. Arms may come down or pull in, loading up for the final release.

✋ Hands

Pulling in or poised — ready to release on 쩔어. Don't anticipate early. Land it ON the word.

Lyric
prep for 쩔어
Phonetic
—
English
spring load

Count-by-Count · Jungkook (8-count phrase 2)

1
jjeoreo · arms pull low Feet together, arms low at sides — reset
▼
COUNT 1
👟 Feet

Feet close or together — narrow stance reset after the wide phrase. Center of gravity drops.

🙌 Body / Arms

Arms pulling low and in. Slight forward lean or hip tilt. Grounded, swagger energy — this phrase is more compact and cool.

✋ Hands

Hands at hip/low-waist level. More chill swagger here — less explosive, more controlled cool.

Lyric
쩔어!
Phonetic
Jjeo-reo!
English
DOPE! 🔥
2
— · step out, arms back Step back R, arms sweep back behind
▼
COUNT 2
👟 Feet

Step or shift — slight weight transfer. Body beginning to open into the next shape.

🙌 Body / Arms

Arms beginning to sweep back. Body leans slightly. This is the transition setting up count 3's shape.

✋ Hands

Active through the transition. Don't let arms go passive between counts.

Lyric
—
Phonetic
—
English
transition
3
— · arms at hip, pop Hip-level arm isolation, body pop
▼
COUNT 3
👟 Feet

Hip-width or slightly wider. Knees soft — this phrase has more bounce and isolation energy.

🙌 Body / Arms

Arms at hip level with a pop or isolation. Body doing a groove/roll element. More street swagger here.

✋ Hands

Hands at hip level, active in the isolation. Wrist/hand energy matters here for the groove feel.

Lyric
—
Phonetic
—
English
groove / pop
4
— · arm sweeps across Wider stance, arm sweeps across front
▼
COUNT 4
👟 Feet

Stance widens slightly. Body beginning to open for the arm sweep.

🙌 Body / Arms

One arm sweeps in a wide arc across the front of the body. The sweep has speed — sharpness in execution here.

✋ Hands

Active through the sweep. Keep the wrist from going floppy on the follow-through.

Lyric
—
Phonetic
—
English
arm sweep
5
— · body opens wide Wide stance, arms wide — open, confident
▼
COUNT 5
👟 Feet

Wide confident stance. Open body position — take up space. This is the midpoint swagger hold.

🙌 Body / Arms

Arms wide and open at roughly shoulder level. Chest open, body facing front. Pure confidence. This is what "swagger" looks like.

✋ Hands

Relaxed but present — not floppy. This is a confident hold, not a tense one.

Lyric
—
Phonetic
—
English
swagger pose 😎
6
— · hip shift + arm drop Hip shifts, one arm drops to side
▼
COUNT 6
👟 Feet

Still wide. Hip shifts to one side — this phrase has groove in it. Let the hips move.

🙌 Body / Arms

One arm drops lower as the hip shifts. Asymmetric shape — more relaxed swagger. V part note: "more swagger, less auk."

✋ Hands

Relaxed but intentional. This is confidence energy, not effort energy. Let it be cool.

Lyric
—
Phonetic
—
English
groove + hip
7
— · step and lean Step out, slight lean, arms settle
▼
COUNT 7
👟 Feet

Step out to one side with a lean. Body settling into the final position setup.

🙌 Body / Arms

Arms settling into a wide relaxed hold. Body leans slightly — casual swagger, not effort. Think Jungkook owning the stage.

✋ Hands

Relaxed, present at the sides or slight outward hold. Confidence comes from ease here.

Lyric
—
Phonetic
—
English
lean + settle
8
쩔어! JJEOREO! 🔥 Wide swagger stance, arms out — OWN IT.
▼
쩔어!
👟 Feet

WIDE strong stance. Both feet grounded and planted. This is the widest, most grounded position in the whole sequence.

🙌 Body / Arms

Arms wide out to the sides at roughly hip/waist level. Chest open, body front-on. FULL confidence. 쩔어 = freaking awesome. Make your body say that.

✋ Hands

Bold wide hold — present, open, grounded. Not shrinking. This is the last thing the audience sees before the next section. Make it land.

Korean
쩔어!
Phonetic
Jjeo-reo!
English
"Dope!" · THE word 🔥

Your Focus Areas · From Today's Notes

①
Retaining MovesPractice is key per Beta. Even 5 min daily on this phrase — say syllables out loud as you move.
②
Active ArmsNot stiff — intentional. Between counts is where arms go dead. Fight that at every transition.
③
Confidence / Swagger"소리쳐봐" = try yelling it. Jin owns this line. So do you. Prep → positivity → growth mindset.
④
Sharpness in ExecutionComes from repetition + knowing the music. Know the syllables = know when to hit.

Practice Notes · Editable

DOPE (쩔어) · BTS · Jin + Jungkook MVP v2 3/21/26
